notes: Maps to Venice's 'Grand Jury Prize' / 'Premio Speciale della Giuria' — the second-tier non-director prize that has been awarded across the full 2000-2025 scope. Despite the category ID name 'silver_lion_grand_jury', this is a SEPARATE award from the 'Silver Lion' — Venice ceremonies typically award both each year. Wikilink target consistent: [[Grand Jury Prize (Venice Film Festival)]]. Display text varies (sometimes 'Grand Special Jury Prize').

Maps to Venice's 'Silver Lion' award (target [[Silver Lion]]). Modern era (2013+): explicitly 'Silver Lion for Best Director' (person-first format). Pre-2013 era: typically awarded as a film+director second-tier prize without 'Best Director' qualifier. Both eras share the same wikilink target so we treat them as one category for tracking continuity.
Distinct from Grand Jury Prize — Venice has both. Special Jury Prize is intermittent (not awarded in some years, including cer 2000). Wikilink target [[Special Jury Prize (Venice Film Festival)]].

display_aliases: Coppa Volpi per la migliore interpretazione maschile
Coppa Volpi per la migliore interpretazione femminile

wikilink_targets: Venice Film Festival Award for Best Screenplay
display_aliases: Golden Osella for Outstanding Technical Contribution (screenplay)
notes: Premio Osella for Best Screenplay. Wikilink target moved between 'Best Screenplay (Venice Film Festival)' and 'Venice Film Festival Award for Best Screenplay' over the years. Older ceremonies (cer 2000+) sometimes use bold-only display 'Golden Osella for Outstanding Technical Contribution (screenplay)' — alias-matching needed.

Awarded to an emerging actor or actress. Has a spotty history at Venice — has been retired and reinstated; Wikipedia coverage gaps possible for some years. If absent from a ceremony's awards section, the scraper produces 0 rows for that category.

display_name: Lion of the Future — Luigi De Laurentiis Award
display_aliases: Luigi De Laurentis Award for a Debut Film
Luigi De Laurentis Award for Debut Feature
notes: Venice's debut-feature prize, equivalent to Cannes Caméra d'Or. Typically appears in a SEPARATE === Lion of the Future === or === Luigi De Laurentis Award === subsection within the Official Awards block; bullets inside that subsection don't carry an inline category wikilink, so the scraper's section-heading-driven Pass 2 (same pattern as Cannes Caméra d'Or) catches it. Spelling varies: 'Laurentis' vs 'Laurentiis'.
